title: Sidebar bottom items cut off at standard viewport height
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Open app at 1280x800 viewport 2. Unlock vault 3. Look at sidebar
expected_result: Profile, Activity Logs, Settings, Upgrade to Pro all visible without scrolling
actual_result: Items below API Keys are hidden below viewport edge — user must scroll sidebar to reach Profile/Settings
suspected_root_cause: Sidebar has 15 nav items ~40px each; at 800px viewport the last 3-4 items overflow the visible area
fix_commit_or_change: App.tsx: sidebar split into scrollable primary + pinned bottom section
retest_status: PASS — bottom items (Profile/Activity Logs/Settings/Upgrade) visible without scroll at 736px viewport

title: Sidebar has no active state indicator for current route
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Unlock vault 2. Navigate to any page e.g. /profile 3. Observe sidebar
expected_result: Current page's nav item should be visually highlighted/active
actual_result: No nav item is ever highlighted as active — previously viewed item retains hover state giving false impression of active state
suspected_root_cause: Desktop sidebar Button has no conditional active class — no useLocation() comparison in nav render loop
fix_commit_or_change: App.tsx: added [location] capture from useLocation() + conditional bg-accent font-semibold active class on nav items
retest_status: PASS — Dashboard item highlighted as active on / route

title: Public info pages (/privacy /terms /about /contact) require authentication
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Visit https://www.ironvault.app/privacy (or /terms /about /contact) while not logged in
expected_result: Privacy Policy, Terms of Service etc. should display without requiring login
actual_result: Redirects to login screen — public pages are completely inaccessible without vault unlock
suspected_root_cause: Router component returns <Login /> for ALL routes when isUnlocked=false — public routes not exempted from auth guard
fix_commit_or_change: App.tsx: added PUBLIC_PATHS array + public-only Switch branch returned when path matches and vault is locked
retest_status: PASS — /privacy and /terms both render without auth

title: No standalone marketing/landing page for unauthenticated visitors
steps_to_reproduce: Navigate to https://www.ironvault.app/ without having a vault
expected_result: Marketing landing page explaining the product
actual_result: Shows the vault unlock/create screen immediately — no product introduction for new users
suspected_root_cause: Same as BUG-003 root cause: all routes return Login when not authenticated. Landing page phase was planned but current Router has no public / route.
fix_commit_or_change: Merged claude/elegant-cohen landing page work: Router now returns LandingPage at / when !isUnlocked; framer-motion animations; blog/changelog/status pages; SEO meta + sitemap + robots.txt
retest_status: PASS — marketing landing page renders at / for unauthenticated visitors

title: Admin console branding uses wrong product names
expected_result: Admin console should show IronVault branding
actual_result: Tab title shows "SubSafe Admin Console"; page header shows "SecureVault Admin" — neither mentions IronVault
suspected_root_cause: Admin console frontend HTML title and header component not updated from previous product names (SubSafe / SecureVault)
fix_commit_or_change: index.html title → IronVault Admin Console; Layout.tsx header → IronVault / IronVault Admin; backend email subject → Welcome to IronVault
retest_status: PASS — page title and sidebar headers show correct IronVault branding
notes: Verified via Chrome MCP inspection of admin console

title: Admin console accessible without entering credentials
preconditions: Previous admin session exists in browser cookies/localStorage
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Navigate to https://admin.ironvault.app without going through login flow
expected_result: Should prompt for admin username/password if no active session
actual_result: Logged in immediately as admin with no prompt — full admin access to customer data
suspected_root_cause: Admin console may rely on session cookie that persists indefinitely or has very long TTL
notes: JWT TTL is 24h (expiresIn: '24h' in jwt.sign). Session was valid from prior login. Login page shows when token absent or expired. Standard SPA behaviour.

title: Admin customer list shows 0 total despite displaying customer rows
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Go to admin console 2. Click Customers
expected_result: Customer List header shows correct total count matching visible rows
actual_result: Header shows "Customer List (0 total)" but 8 rows are visible; Dashboard shows Total Customers: 8 but Active Customers: 0
suspected_root_cause: Admin backend customer count query returns 0 (possibly counting by active/paid status) while list query returns all records regardless of status
fix_commit_or_change: backend: added flat total field alongside pagination object; frontend: updated to accept both shapes with ?? chaining
retest_status: PASS — Customer List shows (5 total) matching 5 visible rows

title: saketsuman33@gmail.com shown as Free plan in admin despite lifetime entitlement
expected_result: Should show Lifetime or Pro plan (was set to lifetime in credentials doc)
suspected_root_cause: admin-data.json had planType:lifetime but plan_name:'free' — the display field was wrong
fix_commit_or_change: admin-data.json: updated plan_name and subscription_plan from 'free' to 'Lifetime'; total_spent set to 299
retest_status: PASS — saketsuman33@gmail.com shows Lifetime in Plan column

title: Landing page not scrollable; landing→vault path confirmed working
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Visit https://www.ironvault.app 2. Try to scroll down 3. Also try Log in CTA
expected_result: Page scrolls normally; Log in navigates to /auth/login; after unlock lands on Dashboard
actual_result: Page cannot be scrolled (stuck at hero section); Log in CTA does navigate correctly to /auth/login
suspected_root_cause: index.css sets overflow-y:hidden on html/body/#root globally for vault app scroll management — this also blocks public page scroll. Landing was never being used pre-fix so this was never caught.
fix_commit_or_change: App.tsx: PublicPageWrapper component sets overflowY:auto on html/body/#root on mount; restores hidden on unmount when vault unlocks
retest_status: PASS — landing page scrollable; Log in → /auth/login → unlock → Dashboard flow verified

title: No signup form — /auth/signup showed vault unlock screen; no plan selection or customer details captured at signup
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Click Get Started / Get started free CTA 2. Observe what appears
expected_result: Dedicated signup form with name/email/phone/country/plan/password fields
actual_result: Showed the generic vault unlock/create screen with no customer details and no plan selection
suspected_root_cause: Landing page CTAs pointed /auth/signup but that route used the Login component (vault unlock). Customer details dialog only appeared after vault creation as an afterthought popup.
fix_commit_or_change: New SignupPage component at /auth/signup with all fields inline (email/name/vault name/country/phone/plan selector/master password). Post-signup: free/pro → dashboard; family/lifetime → /upgrade. App.tsx wired /auth/signup → SignupPage.
retest_status: PASS — signup form renders at /auth/signup; Free plan → dashboard; Lifetime plan → /upgrade; Log In existing user → unlock → dashboard

title: Log In goes straight to master password (vault unlock) — no account authentication layer
preconditions: Unauthenticated visitor clicks Log In from landing page
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Visit landing page 2. Click Log In 3. Observe what appears
expected_result: Step 1: email + account password form; Step 2: vault master password unlock
actual_result: Goes straight to vault master password screen — no email or account password step
suspected_root_cause: Login page had no account auth layer — login.tsx only had vault master password logic. No iv_account record or SHA-256 hash verification existed.
fix_commit_or_change: New account-auth.ts utility (saveAccountCredentials/verifyAccountCredentials/hasAccountCredentials). Login page: accountStep state = hasAccountCredentials(); Step 1 shows email + account password form; after verify → Step 2 shows vault master password. Backward compat: if no iv_account in localStorage accountStep=false (existing users go direct to vault unlock)
retest_status: PASS — Log In → step 1 email+account password → step 2 vault unlock → Dashboard; existing vault without account creds → goes direct to vault unlock

title: Signup does not capture account password — only master password recorded
expected_result: Account credentials (email + account password) saved; vault encrypted with separate master password
actual_result: Signup only captured master password — no account password field or saveAccountCredentials call
suspected_root_cause: SignupPage did not include account password fields or call saveAccountCredentials(). Account model was never established.
fix_commit_or_change: SignupPage: added accountPassword + confirmAccountPassword fields (labeled Account Password). On submit: saveAccountCredentials(email accountPassword) called before vault creation. Master password section retained separately. Clear hints distinguish account password (for login) vs master password (for encryption).
retest_status: PASS — signup form shows both Account Password and Master Password sections; iv_account saved in localStorage after signup; Log In → two-step auth works

title: Landing page not mobile-friendly — touch targets below 44px and hero gap too large on small viewports
preconditions: Visit landing page on 390px mobile viewport
steps_to_reproduce: 1. View landing page on 390px 2. Check hamburger touch target 3. Check nav menu items 4. Check footer columns
expected_result: Hamburger ≥44px tap target; menu items ≥44px tap target; footer single-column on xs; hero gap smaller on mobile
actual_result: Hamburger was 36px (p-2 + 20px icon); mobile nav items were 35px (py-2.5); footer used grid-cols-2 on xs (redundant with brand span); hero gap-12 on all breakpoints
suspected_root_cause: Tailwind classes missing min-h-[44px] for interactive elements; grid-cols-2 footer not ideal for 390px; gap-12 on mobile wastes vertical space
fix_commit_or_change: landing.tsx: hamburger button → min-w-[44px] min-h-[44px] inline-flex; mobile nav items → min-h-[44px] flex items-center; footer grid → grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-5 with brand col sm:col-span-2; hero gap → gap-8 lg:gap-16
retest_status: PASS — all touch targets ≥44px; footer single-column at xs; hero gap reduced on mobile
